title: Account page — change display name form
description: Wire up the Display Name section on the Account page so users can update their display name.
acceptanceCriteria: Display Name section shows a text input pre-filled with the current user name
User can edit the name and click Save
Saving calls PATCH /api/user/profile and updates the name in AuthContext
Updated name is immediately reflected in the top bar username on /dashboard
Empty name shows validation error 'Name is required'
Save button shows loading state during API call
Success shows a brief success message or toast

steps.details: In AccountPage.tsx or a sub-component, use React Hook Form with Zod: z.object({ name: z.string().min(1, 'Name is required') }). Pre-fill the input with user.name from useAuth(). Render an Input and a Save Button with loading state.

onSubmit: call updateProfile({ name }) from apiService. On success: update the user in AuthContext (add an updateUser(user: User) method to AuthContext if not present), show success feedback (e.g. set a success state that renders a green 'Saved!' message for 2 seconds). On error: show error message.

Go to /account, change the name, click Save. Take screenshot of success state. Navigate to /dashboard — verify new name appears in top bar. Go back to /account — verify input shows new name. Try empty name and save — verify validation error.

technicalNotes: Add updateUser(user: User) to AuthContext to allow updating user state after profile changes
The success message should auto-dismiss after 2-3 seconds using setTimeout + state
